title: astragalus dependens var. aurantiacus
intro: Astragalus dependens var. aurantiacus, also known as the orange milk vetch or golden milk vetch, is a plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as enhancing immune function, reducing stress and fatigue, and improving cardiovascular health. Astragalus dependens var. aurantiacus can be prepared for herbal use through methods like drying, decocting, or encapsulation, and it is often consumed as a dietary supplement in the form of capsules, tablets, or teas.
